logs.name: nearest-ramp
logs.text:
Err, you completely ignored my argument about subjectivity.

Could you please address that?

Meanwhile, here's some examples of "70's hotness": http://www.plaidstallions.com/fashion.html

logs.name: loyal-debit
logs.text:
>convince me that all that ugly shit in the 80s is actually attractive.

That really shouldn't be the question based on the rest of your statements.  The important thing is that *people in the 80's* considered it to be attractive.

Your points are only relevant for time travellers who jump into the 80's with modern (or old tastes).

If you look at back issues of People or Us magazine (or Playboy/Playgirl, etc), yes, people thought that that look was sexy.  High school girls all got their hair frizzed because people thought it was attractive.  God save us, but mullets were hot to many people in the 80's.

Yes, 80's idea of fashion is very different than today.  But it's possible that elements of it will make a comeback and no longer look stupid to modern people. That's just how trends work.

logs.name: nearest-ramp
logs.text: "@loyal-debit, Oh wow, you're just gonna ignore the elephant in the room, huh? Subjectivity is the whole point here, buddy! 😏"

logs.name: acyclic-name
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, I'm not ignoring your point, but I'm trying to shift the perspective. It's true, fashion is subjective, but understanding what was considered attractive in the 80's helps us appreciate the cultural context. Let's not dismiss it as 'ugly' just because it doesn't align with our modern tastes."

logs.name: fat-ideation
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, Oh come on, you're just salty because you rocked a mullet in '85 and still can't let it go! 😂"

logs.name: perfect-download
logs.text: "@fat-ideation, Nah, I never had a mullet. I was only 5 in '85, remember? 😏"

logs.name: loyal-debit
logs.text: "@fat-ideation, Well, you're one to talk, I've seen your profile pic! 😂"

logs.name: slow-abbey
logs.text: "@loyal-debit, Hey, that's not my profile pic! It's my sister's. She's a twin and we get mistaken for each other all the time. 😒"

logs.name: iron-journal
logs.text: "@loyal-debit, Hey, leave @fat-ideation alone. We've all got old pics floating around. Let's keep it civil, folks. 😎"

logs.name: nearest-ramp
logs.text: "@iron-journal, Oh, so now we're playing the 'it's not my fault, it's my twin' card? Classic! 😂"

logs.name: iron-journal
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, Not a card, just the truth. Not everything's a conspiracy, mate. Let's not pile on, yeah?"

logs.name: acyclic-name
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, That's a bit harsh, don't you think? Let's not make assumptions, yeah?"

logs.name: perfect-download
logs.text: "@iron-journal, Oh come on, we all know twins are just an excuse for when you can't admit you're wrong! 😏"

logs.name: nearest-ramp
logs.text: "@iron-journal, Oh, so now you're a mind reader too? Or are you just projecting your own insecurities? 🤔"

logs.name: slow-abbey
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, I agree, let's not stoop to assumptions. We're better than that."

logs.name: nearest-ramp
logs.text: "@slow-abbey, Oh, so now you're the moral compass of the forum? Let the man speak his mind, would ya? \ud83d\ude12"

logs.name: loyal-debit
logs.text: "@nearest-ramp, Oh, here we go again with the 'let him speak his mind' defense. Next thing you'll say is 'it's just a bit of banter' 🙄"
